title: Return window and restocking fees for devices
summary: The standard return window, what qualifies for a full refund, and when a restocking fee applies.
symptoms: customer wants to return a phone they just received
asks whether they can still return after 14 days
common_causes: device does not meet customer expectations
customer received a different color or storage option than ordered
customer found a better promo after ordering
resolution_steps: Confirm the return window: 14 days from the DELIVERY date, not the order date.
Full refund is available when the device is in original condition, in the original box, with all accessories and proof of purchase.
A 10% restocking fee applies when the box is opened but the device is otherwise unused. A 20% restocking fee applies if the device shows any wear.
Devices with activated SIMs or transferred phone numbers must be deactivated before return — refer the customer to the technical specialist to port out.
Provide the return label via the escalation contact; the agent does NOT generate return labels directly.
escalation_policy: Escalate to orders-ops when the customer is outside the 14-day window or wants a restocking fee waiver.

title: Shipping SLAs and expedited shipping options
summary: Standard and expedited shipping windows, carrier selection, and delivery guarantees.
symptoms: customer asks when their order will arrive
common_causes: customer placed a standard order but needs the device sooner
customer lives in a remote area and wants a realistic ETA
resolution_steps: Standard shipping: free, 3-5 business days via UPS Ground. Eligible for orders placed before 2 PM ET on a business day.
Expedited shipping: $15 flat, 1-2 business days via UPS Next Day Air. Must be selected at checkout; cannot be upgraded after the order is placed.
Orders placed on Saturday, Sunday, or a US federal holiday ship the next business day.
Alaska, Hawaii, and US territories: add 2-3 business days to standard; expedited is 2-3 business days and costs $25.
PO boxes and APO/FPO addresses: UPS cannot deliver; fall back to USPS Priority Mail (3-7 business days).
escalation_policy: Escalate to orders-ops if a tracked shipment is more than 2 business days past the ETA.

title: Activation requirements when ordering a new device
summary: Which new-device orders require service activation and how activation timing works.
symptoms: customer ordered a phone — asks how to activate
customer wants to order a phone to use on another carrier
common_causes: new customer ordering both a device and a line of service
existing customer upgrading their phone on an existing line
customer intends to use the device unlocked on a different carrier
resolution_steps: New-customer orders (device + new line): activation happens at first boot via the on-screen eSIM prompt; no additional step needed.
Upgrade orders (device on existing line): the SIM transfer happens automatically when the new device powers on with the existing account's eSIM profile. The old device is deactivated within 24 hours.
Unlocked/BYO-carrier orders: the device ships with NO carrier lock. The customer is responsible for activation on their own carrier — do NOT promise any activation support beyond this.
Warn the customer that attempting activation before the order's status is 'delivered' may fail — wait for delivery confirmation.
escalation_policy: Escalate to technical specialist for eSIM activation issues once the device is delivered but not activating.

title: Device compatibility — which plans work with which devices
summary: Which devices work on which network bands, 5G capability, and plan-level restrictions.
symptoms: customer asks if their existing phone will work on our network
customer wants 5G but is on a plan that doesn't include it
common_causes: customer has an older phone without modern network bands
customer is on a legacy plan that doesn't include 5G service
resolution_steps: All devices in the current catalog are compatible with our network across all plans — do not doubt compatibility of a catalog device.
For customer-owned (BYO) devices, check the IMEI via the BYO device checker tool (handled by the technical specialist) — orders specialist does not have this lookup.
5G service requires: (1) a 5G-capable device, (2) a 5G-enabled plan (Ultimate Bundle, 5G Unlimited Mobile, or Gigabit Pro), and (3) coverage in the customer's area.
Plans that include 5G: all current catalog plans. Legacy plans without 5G: 'Basic Talk & Text' and 'Unlimited 4G' — recommend a plan upgrade (via the sales flow) if 5G is desired.
escalation_policy: Hand off to technical specialist for BYO compatibility checks against IMEI.

title: Order status meanings and tracking updates
summary: What each order status means and how tracking progresses.
symptoms: customer wants to know what 'placed' vs 'shipped' means
customer sees no tracking update after 48 hours
common_causes: order is still in the warehouse queue
carrier scan happened but tracking portal is delayed
resolution_steps: Order statuses: 'placed' (payment confirmed, not yet in the warehouse), 'shipped' (carrier has a tracking number and scan), 'delivered' (carrier marked delivered), 'cancelled'.
Call order_shipment_status to fetch the current carrier + tracking number when available.
Expect 12-48 hours between 'placed' and 'shipped'; tracking numbers appear on the label stage, not at order creation.
If tracking has had no scan for more than 48 hours since 'shipped', escalate to orders-ops — this is a lost shipment signal.
Never invent an ETA; always cite the value the order_get tool returned.
escalation_policy: Escalate to orders-ops for no-scan shipments > 48 hours past the 'shipped' status.

title: Cancel or modify an order after placement
summary: Window in which an order can be cancelled or modified, and what happens to the payment.
symptoms: customer placed the wrong item and wants to change
customer wants to cancel an order they just placed
common_causes: customer picked the wrong color/storage/SKU
customer found a better deal
customer's plans changed
resolution_steps: An order can be cancelled any time while status is 'placed'. Once 'shipped', it CANNOT be cancelled — it must be returned using the return policy once delivered.
Look up the order with order_get to confirm current status.
If still 'placed', use propose_cancel_order; the customer click performs the actual cancellation.
Order modifications (changing SKU, address, payment method) are NOT supported — cancel and re-place is the only path.
Refunds for cancelled orders: card payments reverse within 5 business days; bank ACH reverses within 7-10 business days.
escalation_policy: Escalate to orders-ops if an address change is needed on an already-shipped order — they can redirect via the carrier.

title: Taxes, fees, and eligibility for in-cart promotions
summary: What's included in the quoted total and which promos apply in-chat vs. web checkout only.
symptoms: customer asks why the quote is higher than the sticker price
customer saw a promo on the website that doesn't appear in chat
common_causes: sales tax varies by ship-to state
web-exclusive trade-in and bundle promos don't apply in chat-based orders
resolution_steps: Always call order_quote to get the authoritative total — it includes item subtotal, state sales tax (flat 8.75% in dev fixtures), and applicable fees.
Shipping is free for standard on orders over $35; $5 flat under $35. Expedited is $15 regardless of total.

Trade-in and device-finance promos are handled only through web checkout — explain this to the customer and point them to the self-service catalog URL via the escalation contact if they need those specific promos.
Bundle-with-a-plan promos work through in-chat ordering; single-device purchase at retail price is what the order specialist quotes by default.
escalation_policy: Point the customer to web checkout (via escalation contact) for trade-in or device-finance promos.

title: Lost or damaged shipment — what to do
summary: Process for filing a claim on a lost or damaged shipment.
symptoms: customer's tracking says 'delivered' but they never received it
common_causes: carrier delivered to the wrong address
package stolen after delivery (porch theft)
package sustained damage in transit
resolution_steps: Confirm the tracking status with order_shipment_status; note the 'delivered' scan time.
For 'delivered but not received': a claim can be filed if the customer notifies us within 5 days of the scan. After 5 days, the carrier claim window closes.
For damaged shipments: customer must take photos of the box + device before returning. Damage claims filed within 7 days of delivery qualify for a free replacement.
The agent does NOT file the claim directly — provide the escalation contact for orders-ops to handle the claim intake.
Offer to propose_cancel_order ONLY if the order is still in 'placed' status — otherwise, the return/claim path is the right one.
escalation_policy: All lost/damaged claims go through orders-ops; provide the escalation contact.
